title: Rodolphe Saadé Billionaires World’s 5 Richest Heirs 2026 Born with a golden spoo...
content:
Rodolphe Saadé Billionaires World’s 5 Richest Heirs 2026 Born with a golden spoon, the world’s richest heirs inherit empires already in motion, stepping directly into global wealth, influence, and legacy. But inheritance alone is not enough: it comes with pressure to protect what was built and expand those empires. Here are the world’s five richest 1010	heirs according to Forbes’ Real-Time Billionaires rankings on May 1, 2026. 1. Rob Walton & family Net worth: $149.7 billion Source of wealth:Walmart Citizenship:American Rob Walton, the eldest son of Walmart founder Sam Walton, played a major role in building the retail giant into one of the world’s largest retailers. He joined Walmart in 1978 and became vice chairman in 1982 and succeeded his father as chairman in 1992. Walton stepped down in 2015 when leadership passed to his son-in- law Greg Penner, but remained on the board until 2024. Since March 2024, his fortune has risen by $72.3 billion to $149.7

Arab Billionaires The Arab Billionaires Backing The World’s Football Clubs Billionaires often back football clubs out of deep personal fandom or as strategic investments. In both cases, passion for the game merges with financial power to shape clubs on and off the 1212	pitch. Here are the five Arab billionaires who are investing in football clubs, and their net worths according to Forbes as of May 6, 2026. Prince Alwaleed Bin Talal Alsaud Citizenship:Saudi Net worth:$21.7 billion Club:Al-Hilal Saudi Club Prince Alwaleed oversees Kingdom Holding Company (KHC), which in April 2026 signed a binding share sale and purchase agreement with the Public Investment Fund (PIF) to acquire a 70% stake in Al-Hilal Club Company for $224 million, implying an enterprise value of $373 million. The PIF has been the club’s primary shareholder since 2023, following the launch of Saudi Arabia’s sports clubs investment and privatization program. Al-Hilal has won 90 official titles, including four AFC Champions League titles and holds the record for the most Saudi Pro League titles, with 21 championships since 1962, most recently in 2024.
